programmes.show_description: Dr Emma McMullen has a painful nail condition to diagnose; Dr Natalia Spierings tries to figure out why a shoulder lump is oily; and Dr Adil Sheraz deals with a mole on a mole.
Boozed-up Britain at its worst, from a bouncer racially abused by a clubber to a roadside rumble between a late-night drinker and a taxi driver whose car has been damaged.
Emergency situations compel Dr Beecraft, Dr Glynne and Dr Swanne to refer their patients for specialist treatment.
Paramedics arrive with eight-month-old Noah, who is struggling to breathe. Kathleen, 78, who fell over, requires a CT scan and stitches for deep wounds by her eye.
Dr Adil Sheraz treats a bus driver with over 100 lipomas, Dr Toby Nelson tries to help a 23-year-old who's covered in keloids, plus a treat of a cyst for Dr Emma McMullen.
The FF9 Friends challenge the Eggheads.

Tribute singer Paige takes on her most ambitious fitness challenge to date - her first ever half marathon. Meanwhile, dog rescuer Susie searches for homes for seven abandoned puppies.
Sunshine Radio presenter Simon juggles early mornings and late nights as he swaps the studio for the stage, performing as Elvis and Frank Sinatra on the Costa Blanca.
Graeme Hall meets Toy Poodle Ralph who, after being hit by a car, has fallen out with owner Karina. And noisy wire-haired Dachshund Tulah clucks like a chicken on every car trip!
The Traffic Cops come up against some of the most dangerous and vicious criminals operating on their North Yorkshire patch.
Documentary series. A violent new crime wave known as County Lines is taking hold in Britain. Across the country, urban drug gangs are expanding their empires into small towns.
A fifth of the offences which North Yorkshire Police deal with, like theft and drug-dealing, are committed by crooks from outside the area.
Jim Woolerton goes hot on the trail of a speeding motorcyclist and Jon Moon reveals his guilty pleasure when he retrieves a stolen caravan. (S8 Ep )
Jim intercepts a car containing cannabis, a late-night reveller talks himself into trouble and Sergeant Baxter is confronted by a dishonest driver.
The interceptors stop an uninsured driver with a violent past. Jon Peacock and his dog Brad hunt for suspected burglars. Sarah Capes smells something illegal.

programmes.show_name: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car...
programmes.show_description: ...Back! Following car recovery companies that are tasked with taking illegal, unroadworthy, untaxed, uninsured vehicles off the road and ensuring their owners pay the penalty.
Harper's guilt surges. Cash struggles with the rules. Sonny's frustration gets the better of him.
Remi pays a long overdue visit. Justin and Leah rekindle the romance. Is Mali right to not trust Holden?
A young couple need confirmation that they are expecting their first baby, and a woman with a history of postnatal depression is worried about her fourth pregnancy.
Four-year-old Skyler is rushed into Paediatrics care with recurring seizures; 65-year-old Shaun has tumbled down some steps; and Eric, 85, has a badly swollen leg.
Shocking new series following medics battling to save the lives of people injured on Britain's deadly roads. In this episode, Martin suffers a catastrophic heart attack at the wheel... (Ep1)
When Duncan is admitted to Royal Stoke emergency department after falling down a full flight of stairs onto concrete, trauma team leader Richard Hall fears the worst.
Twenty-six-year-old Curtis has a condition that's been troubling him since he was ten. Plus, an operation to remove a facial cyst that's lying close to the temporal nerve.
In Swindon, 60-year-old Tom Rogers was brutally stabbed by a stranger. Investigators piece together every shred of evidence to see whether the killer has attacked before.
Two recently treated patients return seeking more urgent help from the hard-pressed doctors.
Paramedics take Shaun, 53, to resus after he collapses at home following multiple seizures. And Margaret, 94, has a high heart rate and dangerously low oxygen levels.
Both Dr Emma McMullen and Dr Adil Sheraz need a wipe down after dealing with cysts, Dr Toby Nelson has a lipoma named after him, and Dr Natalia Spierings performs a mole removal.
Does team WHAT have what it takes to overcome the Eggheads?

Caravan sales queen Bambi organises a beach-themed birthday bash for her best friend Jojo on the Costa del Sol, while Diane and Jim host a British Legion fundraiser in Benidorm.
Former lorry driver Shane has big plans for his Costa Del Sol bar with his ex Chrissy. Meanwhile, Paul takes on a big catering job for a venue's 20th anniversary in Benidorm.
A wayward Jack Russell is ruining the relationship between a little girl and her grandad. And Graeme Hall revisits a couple whose pair of puppy sisters have returned to warring ways.
In York, early-morning raids are carried out on two properties seeking suspects who are believed to be exploiting teenage girls.
A tyre stinger is used to stop a vehicle suspected of carrying stolen phones. Automatic Number Plate Recognition is used to track and intercept a vehicle.
A van driver is twice the legal limit and has three bags of white powder in his pocket. A public-spirited motorist clues the police into a drunk-driving woman.
The hunt for a suspected burglar takes an unexpected turn, a late-night stop reveals a bag of drugs and Jon has a tussle with a fallen tree.
Dan is in hot pursuit of a two-wheeled tearaway, a drunk driver nearly causes a calamity and Mark deals with a three-car collision.
Dog handler Martyn finds himself in the firing line when he cops a tasty right hook and Dan is given the runaround by an uninsured driver.

Remi starts radiation therapy. Will Lacey see through Holden's lies? Justin romances Leah.
Stomach complaints keep the Balham Park GPs on their toes, but they suspect that nothing more than chicken nuggets and broccoli are to blame.

programmes.show_name: Call the Bailiffs: Time to Pay Up!
programmes.show_description: Mark and Sam chase a £7,000 debt over a car sold with disputed mileage, leading to seized luxury cars, tense negotiations, and claims of damage to an enforcement agent's vehicle.
Comedy drama starring Hugh Grant and Toni Collette. A friendship develops between a bright but awkward 12-year-old boy and a wayward thirtysomething man. (2002)
A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.
